headline: Walmart closes stores in key growing market, customers concerned
summary: It is common for large retailers to close and open locations from time to time as they restructure and assess the profitability of each store. A supermarket that thrives in a high-traffic hub might struggle in a saturated market, leading it to quietly close underperforming stores. Today, Walmart ...

headline: Goldman Sachs spots Nvidia-linked shift not seen in 13 years
summary: Smart money is getting a lot more cautious on the stock market’s finest. According to TipRanks, Goldman Sachs said that hedge funds sold off stocks in March at the fastest pace in 13 years, a rare shift pointing to growing anxiety across markets. The change hit high-profile names, including Nvidia ...

headline: MAC.BID Brings Deep Retail Discounts to El Paso, Texas with Online Auctions Starting at $1
summary: Online retail liquidation company, MAC.BID, announced today the company is opening its 29th warehouse location in El Paso, Texas. Located at 12435 Rojas Drive, MAC.BID will open later this spring and plans to hire approximately 80 new teammates across various roles and functions.

headline: For 13th consecutive year, Walmart, Sam's Club and Feeding America partner for 'Fight Hunger. Spark Change.' hunger relief campaign
summary: The Fight Hunger. Spark Change. campaign returns for its 13th year, uniting all U.S. Walmart and Sam's Club locations along with their customers, members, suppliers and associates around a shared goal: ensuring neighbors facing hunger across the country have access to the nutritious food they need to thrive.

headline: Small Acts, Big Impact: Walmart and Sam’s Club Kick Off 13th Annual ‘Fight Hunger. Spark Change.’
summary: In-store or online, North Texas residents can turn everyday shopping into local meals for families in need.Dallas, TX, April 06, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- For the 13th consecutive year, all U.S. Walmart stores and Sam’s Club locations are launching the Fight Hunger. Spark Change. campaign, including right here in North Texas.
